subject: Business Studies
chapter: BS06 — Staffing (Need, Process, Recruitment Sources, Selection, Training and Development)

questions.question: Nova Motors operates a manufacturing plant and has expanded rapidly. Because of vacancies and rising workload, some departments are unable to keep operations smooth. Which staffing importance is being highlighted most directly?
options: Ensuring continuity of operations by placing the right people in the right jobs
Fixing the product price before sales begin
Deciding the amount of long-term debt the firm should raise
Recording only the cash transactions of the firm
questions.explanation:
Staffing is needed so that every role is filled with capable people at the right time.
When vacancies interrupt work, the key concern is continuity of operations and effective deployment of employees.
The other options relate to finance or accounting, not the staffing function.
Hence, the correct answer is the option that focuses on matching people with jobs.

questions.question: In Nova Motors, the HR department plans manpower, recruits candidates, selects them, and later arranges training for new employees. Which statement best shows staffing as a part of Human Resources Management?
options: Staffing is limited to preparing monthly payroll only
Staffing is an integrated HRM function that links workforce planning, recruitment, selection, placement and training
Staffing is unrelated to employee development after appointment
Staffing begins only after the firm closes its recruitment drive
questions.explanation:
Human Resources Management is broader than hiring alone.
Staffing becomes a part of HRM because it works together with planning, recruitment, selection, placement, induction and training.
Payroll, in contrast, is an accounting or administrative activity, not the full meaning of staffing.
Therefore the integrated HRM view is the correct one.

questions.question: Nova Motors has estimated manpower requirements and identified several openings. It has also collected applications from the market. What should normally come next in the staffing process before placement?
questions.explanation:
The staffing process generally moves from manpower planning to recruitment and then to selection.
Only after candidates are selected can the firm proceed to placement and orientation.
The other options are not part of the staffing process.
So the next logical step after receiving applications is selection.

questions.question: Nova Motors has announced vacancies and received hundreds of applications. Management now wants to attract more suitable candidates from the labour market. Which activity is recruitment?
options: Choosing the most suitable applicant after tests and interviews
Attracting and encouraging potential candidates to apply for vacant posts
Assigning a new employee to a specific desk after joining
Improving the current employee's future managerial potential
questions.explanation:
Recruitment means creating a pool of interested applicants for the vacant posts.
Selection starts only after recruitment and involves choosing the best person from that pool.
Placement is assignment after appointment, while development focuses on future potential.
Hence the activity of attracting applicants is recruitment.

questions.question: In Nova Motors, a senior supervisor retires and the firm promotes one of its experienced assistants to the vacant post. Which recruitment source has been used, and what is its main advantage?
options: External source; it always brings very large numbers of applicants
Internal source; it improves morale through promotion opportunities
External source; it removes the need for selection
Internal source; it eliminates all training needs automatically
questions.explanation:
Promotions and transfers are internal sources of recruitment.
Their major merit is that they motivate existing employees and improve morale by offering career growth.
They do not remove selection or training needs completely.
Therefore the scenario clearly shows internal recruitment.

questions.question: Nova Motors wants fresh design ideas and new market knowledge for a newly created post. It decides to hire from outside the organization. Which statement best fits this choice?
options: It may increase the pool of talent and bring fresh ideas, though it can be costlier and slower
It always ensures faster promotion for existing employees
It completely avoids the need for interviews and tests
It is useful only when there are no vacancies
questions.explanation:
External recruitment brings in candidates from outside the organisation.
Its advantage is a wider choice and new ideas, which is useful for new or specialised jobs.
However, it often takes more time and money than internal recruitment.
So the first option captures the real merit and limitation correctly.

questions.question: Nova Motors has introduced a new software module. It wants employees to learn while performing actual work under the guidance of a senior colleague. Which training method is being used?

The learner gains skill by doing real work instead of only studying theory.
Lectures, case studies and conferences are generally off-the-job or classroom-based methods.
Hence coaching is the correct answer.

questions.question: Nova Motors wants new supervisors to discuss decision-making problems in a classroom before handling live operations. Which training method is most appropriate?

It is useful when the organisation wants skill-building with limited operational risk.
Job rotation and apprenticeship are on-the-job methods, while transfer is not a training method.
So vestibule training fits the situation best.

questions.question: Nova Motors sends some employees for short-term skill improvement and also prepares a few high-potential staff for future leadership roles. Which statement is correct?
options: Training focuses on current job needs, while development prepares employees for future growth and broader responsibilities
Development is only for factory labourers, while training is only for managers
Training and development are identical in every respect
Development removes the need for recruitment
questions.explanation:
Training is mainly job-oriented and helps employees perform their present tasks better.
Development is more future-oriented and prepares employees for higher responsibility and long-term growth.
The two are related but not identical.
Therefore the first statement is correct.

questions.question: Nova Motors needs to fill a senior post quickly and already has an experienced employee who knows the firm's systems. Which recruitment source is most suitable, and why?
options: External source, because it is always cheaper
Internal source, because it is faster and the employee already understands the organisation
External source, because it removes the need for placement
Internal source, because it guarantees no training is ever needed
questions.explanation:
When an experienced insider is available, internal recruitment is often faster and easier.
The employee already knows the organisation, reducing adjustment time and risk.
It does not completely eliminate training, but it is suitable when speed and familiarity matter.
Hence the internal source is the best fit here.

questions.question: In Nova Motors, HR first places a vacancy notice, then receives applications, and only after that evaluates the candidates. Which part of the staffing process is represented by the vacancy notice and application collection?
questions.explanation:
Recruitment is the process of attracting and encouraging applicants to apply for vacancies.
Vacancy notice and collection of applications both belong to recruitment.
Evaluation of candidates comes later under selection.
So the correct answer is recruitment.

questions.question: Nova Motors has installed advanced machinery and notices more errors when workers use the new system. Which reason best explains the need for training?
options: To help employees learn updated skills and reduce mistakes in a changed work environment
To decide the firm's dividend payout ratio
To avoid any recruitment in the future
questions.explanation:
Training becomes necessary when technology, work methods or safety requirements change.
Employees need updated skills so that errors decline and productivity improves.
The other options are unrelated to training and development.
Thus the main need is skill improvement for the new system.
